original_hypothesis.title: Admin Privilege Abuse: Unbounded Gasless Basefee Drain
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setGaslessBasefee` function does not enforce any maximum limit on the base fee. A compromised admin could set an astronomically high base fee. When users execute pending meta-transactions, the trusted forwarder will deduct this massive fee, instantly draining the users' balances.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Users sign meta-transactions authorizing a specific transfer amount, but they do not sign the base fee amount. An admin changing the base fee after the signature but before execution can extract unbounded value from the user.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No upper bound check on newGaslessBasefee

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin sets base fee to 1,000,000 EURF. Relayer executes pending meta-tx. User loses 1,000,000 EURF.
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker (admin/relayer) drains entire user balances via the base fee mechanism.

economic_questions: Are meta-transaction signatures bound to a specific base fee off-chain?
Is there a timelock on base fee changes?
validation_requirements: Set massive base fee, execute valid meta-tx, verify user balance is drained

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Total balance of all users executing meta-transactions

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
